description: Add statsmodels for time-series diagnostics — GitHub trovlunde/stock_analyzer#9

userStories.title: Add statsmodels dependency and diagnostics package skeleton
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want statsmodels installed via uv with a dedicated diagnostics package and documented use cases so researchers know when to opt in.
acceptanceCriteria: `statsmodels` added via `uv add`; importable in CI without network
New `stock_analysis/diagnostics/` package exists with module docstring describing stationarity and regression use cases
README or AGENTS.md notes that diagnostics are opt-in — not used by daily job or Flask API
No new imports of diagnostics in `jobs/daily.py`, `app.py`, or paper-trading modules
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: Stationarity diagnostics (ADF) for Series and feature frames
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want ADF stationarity checks on feature columns before adding them to classifiers so I can spot non-stationary inputs early.
acceptanceCriteria: `check_stationarity(series, *, alpha=0.05)` wraps `statsmodels.tsa.stattools.adfuller` and returns dict with statistic, p_value, is_stationary
`check_stationarity_frame(df, columns=None)` returns a DataFrame report for numeric feature columns
Handles short series gracefully (clear error or insufficient_data flag — no crash on empty input)
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: Regression diagnostics helper for prepared feature frames
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want OLS regression diagnostics on feature vs target columns so I can sanity-check linear relationships before training classifiers.
acceptanceCriteria: `regression_diagnostics(X, y)` fits OLS via statsmodels on aligned numeric DataFrame/Series
Returns structured dict with r_squared, coefficients, durbin_watson, and condition_number (or equivalent summary fields)
Rejects or drops non-numeric columns with clear behaviour documented in docstring
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: CLI diagnose subcommand on prepared classification data
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a CLI entry point that runs stationarity and regression diagnostics on prepared movement-classifier features so I do not need ad-hoc notebooks.
acceptanceCriteria: `stock-analysis-evaluate diagnose` subcommand (or equivalent script entry in pyproject.toml) accepts ticker/period args consistent with other evaluate commands
Command prints stationarity report for feature columns and regression diagnostics vs return/target column from `prepare_classification_data` output
Uses existing data-prep helpers; tests mock `get_index_data` — no live yfinance in pytest
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: P2 pytest: synthetic stationarity and regression diagnostics
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want regression tests on synthetic series so ADF and OLS helpers behave predictably without network access.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: white-noise series flagged stationary; random-walk series flagged non-stationary at alpha=0.05
Test: synthetic linear X→y yields R² near 1.0 and expected slope sign
Test: CLI diagnose smoke test with mocked index data returns exit code 0
No network access in CI
uv run pytest passes
